div1.entryFree:
Insinuation, 1) the act of making favour with a person: make compromise, i., parley and base truce to arms invasive, John V, 1, 68.
2) meddling: their defeat does by their own i. grow, Hml. V, 2, 59.
3) intimation, suggestion: most barbarous intimation! yet a kind of i., as it were, in via, in way, of explication, LLL IV, 2, 14 (Holofernes' speech).
